content.full_text:
UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA

FPPC
Mar 14 11:01 AM '90

BERKELEY • DAVIS • IRVINE • LOS ANGELES • RIVERSIDE • SAN DIEGO • SAN FRANCISCO SANTA BARBARA • SANTA CRUZ

DAVID PIERPONT GARDNER
President

OFFICE OF THE PRESIDENT
300 LAKESIDE DRIVE
OAKLAND, CALIFORNIA 94612-3550

RONALD W. BRADY
Senor Vice President —
Administration

May 7, 1990

Ms. Jeanette E. Turvill
Political Reform Consultant,
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Jeanette:

Every two years my office updates and publishes the University's
"Political Reform Act Disqualification Requirements" pamphlet
(enclosed) to be distributed to all University employees. We are
scheduling a newly revised edition to be published and distributed in June, 1990.

Other than updating the Coordinators' names and addresses, the
only substantive change we determined was the reference to the
material financial effect regulations effective November, 1988
(18702 through 18702.6). We would appreciate your comments
regarding any additional revisions that may need to be brought to
our attention.

Enclosed for your review is a copy of the pamphlet with our
proposed changes in red ink. For reference, the numbers in blue
ink refer to the appropriate Sections of the Code and Regulations.

Could we please have your comments within two weeks? We are
under a time constraint to forward the pamphlet for publishing by
June 1, in order to charge the costs to our current fiscal year
budget.

Sincerely,

Afton E. Crooks
University Conflict of
Interest Code Coordinator

Enclosure

sections.question_synthetic: The University of California is requesting FPPC review and comments on a revised Political Reform Act Disqualification Requirements pamphlet scheduled for publication in June 1990, specifically seeking feedback on updates to material financial effect regulations and other substantive changes.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response/advice letter. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present. Document requests feedback within a two-week timeframe. The letter includes a time constraint for publishing by June 1990.
